patient_context.chief_complaint: sudden onset right-sided pleuritic chest pain and dyspnea
patient_context.hpi: Tall thin male with sudden sharp right chest pain onset 2 hours ago at rest. No trauma. No prior similar episodes.

notes: Classic spontaneous pneumothorax presentation. Expect CXR to show right apical pleural line.

patient_context.hpi: 64-year-old male on long-haul flight 3 days ago. Now with acute onset dyspnea, mild pleuritic pain, and hypoxemia.

notes: High probability PE. CXR may show Hampton's hump or Westermark sign.

patient_context.chief_complaint: productive cough, fever, and right lower lobe opacity on CXR
patient_context.hpi: 3-day history of productive cough with yellow sputum, fever to 39.2°C, right-sided pleuritic pain.

notes: Lobar pneumonia pattern expected. Differential includes aspiration vs atypical organisms.

patient_context.chief_complaint: worsening dyspnea on exertion and bilateral leg swelling
patient_context.hpi: Known HFrEF (EF 35%), 5-day history of worsening dyspnea, 3-pillow orthopnea, bilateral leg edema.

notes: Bilateral Kerley B lines, cardiomegaly, pleural effusions expected on CXR.

patient_context.chief_complaint: incidental right upper lobe mass on routine CXR
patient_context.hpi: 40 pack-year smoking history. Routine pre-op CXR showed 3.2cm right upper lobe spiculated mass.

notes: High suspicion for primary lung carcinoma. CT chest + PET-CT and biopsy workup needed.
